entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Most popular Sunny engine; 16V DOHC design delivering 110hp in B13/B14 models - timing chain issues dominate replacement demand.

Crank position sensor failure causing no-start
Knock sensor issues leading to retarded timing

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: B13/B14 1.4L EFI unit; known for overheating and head gasket failures driving many owners to swap to GA16DE.

Weak original tensioner exacerbating chain wear

commonProblems.h2: Nissan Sunny Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan Sunny's GA series engines are generally robust but timing chain stretch on GA16DE/GA14DE units and overheating-related head gasket failure on the 1.4L GA14DE dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded timing components and gaskets designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - Why GA Series Sunny Engines Develop Rattle
problems.rootCause: Original tensioner design loses pressure over time, allowing chain to stretch and jump timing marks - exacerbated by infrequent oil changes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New chain, guides, tensioner fitted before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before valve damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt GA16DE with upgraded chain system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or already-failed units

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative chain kit from a specialist offers good value. Once the chain has jumped or you've lost power, replacement with a rebuilt unit including upgraded components is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt GA16DE engine prices for your Nissan Sunny

problems.rootCause: Cooling system design combined with thin head gasket material leads to overheating under load; coolant enters cylinders causing misfire and eventual hydro-lock risk.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head skim, new multi-layer gasket, coolant system flush
Complete rebuilt GA14DE or GA16DE swap with upgraded cooling, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended - many owners swap to GA16DE for reliability

problems.recommendation: GA14DE overheating often causes secondary damage. Unless the failure was caught immediately, investing in a rebuilt GA16DE swap (compatible with minimal wiring changes) provides better long-term reliability than repairing the problematic 1.4L unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for GA14DE to GA16DE engine swap for your Nissan Sunny

problems.h4: Crank/Knock Sensor Failure - Retarded Timing & Power Loss
problems.rootCause: Age-related sensor degradation causes ECU to retard ignition timing as a safety measure - resulting in hesitation, poor fuel economy and limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, replace faulty crank/knock sensor, clear codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if sensors are the sole issue

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit with new sensors, wiring harness check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if multiple electrical faults or high mileage

problems.recommendation: Start with sensor diagnostics - a £200 sensor fix beats a £2,000 engine if that's the only fault. But if your Sunny has 100k+ miles and other age-related issues, a rebuilt engine with all new sensors is better value long-term.
problems.cta: Compare GA series sensor-ready rebuilt engines for your Nissan Sunny

problems.affectedModels: Nissan Sunny B13/B14 1.5L GA15DS carburettor variants (1990-1996)
problems.rootCause: Carburettor jets and diaphragms harden with age; ethanol-blended fuel accelerates corrosion causing poor idle, stalling and fuel consumption spikes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean, replace jets/diaphragms, bench test
repairOptions.longevity: Good option for low-mileage, well-maintained engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt GA15DS with new carb or EFI conversion option, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage or if considering EFI upgrade

problems.recommendation: If your GA15DS otherwise runs well, a specialist carb rebuild is cost-effective. But if you're already experiencing multiple issues, consider a rebuilt unit - some owners use this opportunity to convert to GA15DE EFI for better driveability.
problems.cta: View rebuilt GA15DS carburettor engine options for your Nissan Sunny

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan Sunny
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain rattle, overheating GA14DE, or sensor failures don't have to mean the end for your Sunny. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Nissan Sunny specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original weak points. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery - get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan Sunny Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan Sunny and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan Sunny Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan Sunny is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.3L DOHC GA13DE powered base-spec B13 and B14 Sunny models from 1990-1998, offering adequate economy but modest power - a reliable unit when maintained but crank sensor failures are a known high-mileage issue.
commonFailurePoints: Crank position sensor failure causing intermittent no-start; idle air control valve sticking at high mileage
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L GA14DE EFI engine appeared in B13 and B14 generations but gained notoriety for overheating and head gasket failures, driving many owners to swap to the more robust 1.6L GA16DE unit.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure due to overheating; weak original timing tensioner exacerbating chain wear
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Offered in both carburettor (GA15DS/ECC) and EFI (GA15DE) forms across B13/B14, plus the rare turbocharged E15ET in B12 models - the carb variants are simpler to swap while the turbo demands specialist attention.
commonFailurePoints: Carb jet clogging on GA15DS; turbo oil seal failure on E15ET; general age-related sensor wear
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship GA16DE 16V DOHC engine delivered 110hp in B13/B14 Super Saloon and Lucino variants - the most sought-after replacement due to performance and parts availability, though timing chain stretch remains the dominant failure mode.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch/rattle at 80k-120k miles; crank/knock sensor failures causing retarded timing
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Sunny 1.6L petrol engines

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The QG18DE 1.8L appeared in late N15 Sunny models as a higher-output option; less common in the UK but valued for torque - timing chain and variable timing system issues mirror the 1.6L QG unit.
commonFailurePoints: Variable timing solenoid faults; timing chain guide wear on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan Sunny 1.8L petrol engines

groups.title: Nissan Sunny Classic RWD Engine Sizes

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smallest A12 OHV engine powered early B310 RWD Sunnys; simple and robust but age-related timing chain and valve train wear are expected on surviving examples.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch causing rattling; valve guide wear leading to oil consumption
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.4L Petrol Engines (Classic)
items.description: The A14 OHV 1.4L was the mainstream B310 engine; beloved by classic collectors but carburettor diaphragms and timing components require attention on 40+ year-old units.
commonFailurePoints: Carburettor diaphragm failure; timing chain guide deterioration; oil pump wear
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny 1.4L petrol engine prices (Classic)

items.title: Nissan Sunny 1.5L Petrol Engines (Classic)
items.description: The largest A15 OHV engine offered in B310 models; robust design but like all classic engines, age-related seal and gasket failure is expected without recent maintenance. Not sure which engine size your Nissan Sunny needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Nissan Sunny specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Oil seal leakage; timing chain tensioner wear; carburettor adjustment drift

fuelTypes.h2: Nissan Sunny Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Nissan Sunny engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early A12 classics to the latest QG16DE units.

items.title: Nissan Sunny Petrol Engines
items.description: All UK-market Nissan Sunny models were petrol-powered, spanning OHV A-series classics through DOHC GA-series workhorses to later QG-series units.
items.descriptor: All UK-market Nissan Sunny models were petrol-powered, spanning OHV A-series classics through DOHC GA-series workhorses to later QG-series units.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan Sunny Petrol Engine Families
families: GA16DE - most common replacement engine (B13/B14 1.6L), timing chain risk
GA14DE - B13/B14 1.4L, known overheating/head gasket issues
GA15DS - carburettor 1.5L, simpler wiring for swaps
E15ET - rare B12 turbo, enthusiast favourite but turbo seal failures
A14/A15 - classic RWD B310 engines, age-related wear expected
foundIn: B12 (1986-1990) - E13, E15, E15ET SOHC engines
B13 (1990-1994) - GA13DE, GA14DE, GA15DS, GA16DE DOHC
B14 (1995-1998) - GA13DE, GA14DE, GA15DE, GA16DE, Lucino variants
knownFor: GA16DE timing chain stretch at 80k-120k miles is the #1 replacement driver
GA14DE overheating/head gasket failures prompt many GA16DE swaps
Carburettor variants (GA15DS) valued for simpler wiring vs EFI conversions
Classic A-series engines robust but require age-related seal/gasket attention
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Nissan Sunny Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Nissan Sunny's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from specialists.

years.description: The rear-wheel-drive B310 established the Sunny's reputation for simple, robust OHV engines beloved by classic collectors today.
keyChanges: Launch of B310 generation with RWD layout
A12, A14, A15 OHV engine family introduced
Carburettor fuel delivery standard across range
knownFor: Simple OHV design easy for DIY maintenance
Timing chain stretch expected on high-mileage survivors
Classic car parts availability good in UK enthusiast networks
Rust protection less advanced than later generations
years.cta: Get quotes for B310 Nissan Sunny replacement engines

years.description: The B12 moved Sunny to front-wheel drive and introduced the new E-series SOHC engines, including the rare turbocharged E15ET.
keyChanges: Platform change: RWD to FWD layout
New E-series SOHC engine family launched
E15ET turbo variant introduced for enthusiast market
knownFor: E15ET turbo sought by enthusiasts but oil seal failures common
E-series generally robust but oil pump wear on high-mileage units
Transition to FWD improved interior space but changed driving character
Early EFI systems on some markets, carburettors still common in UK

GA14DE 1.4L introduced but later gained overheating reputation
knownFor: GA16DE timing chain stretch at 80k-120k miles is the #1 failure mode
GA14DE overheating/head gasket issues drive many GA16DE swaps
GA15DS carburettor valued for simpler wiring vs EFI conversions
B13 remains highly popular with UK classic/import enthusiasts

knownFor: GA14DE overheating remained a key reliability concern
GA16DE timing chain issues persisted from B13
Lucino variants sought by JDM enthusiasts for styling
B14 represents peak of GA-series Sunny development

faq.h2: Nissan Sunny Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engine does a Nissan Sunny have?
items.answer: The Nissan Sunny used multiple petrol engine families across generations: A-series OHV (B310), E-series SOHC (B12), GA-series DOHC (B13/B14), and QG-series (N15). The GA16DE 1.6L is the most common replacement request.
keyPoints: B13/B14 generations dominate UK replacement demand
GA16DE, GA14DE, GA15DS are the most searched engine codes
All UK Sunny models were petrol; no factory diesel variants
Enter your registration to confirm your exact engine code instantly
items.cta: Compare Nissan Sunny engine codes and prices now

items.question: Why is my Nissan Sunny GA16DE making a rattling noise on cold start?
items.answer: A rattling noise on cold start in GA16DE engines typically indicates timing chain stretch or tensioner failure - the most common failure mode for B13/B14 Sunny models.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch occurs at 80k-120k miles on GA16DE units
Ignoring the rattle risks chain jump, valve damage and engine failure
Rebuilt GA16DE engines include upgraded chain kits addressing the weakness
Get a diagnostic check before the fault escalates to catastrophic failure
items.cta: Get quotes for GA16DE rebuilt engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: Is the Nissan Sunny GA14DE 1.4L engine reliable?
items.answer: The GA14DE has a documented tendency for overheating and head gasket failure, leading many owners to swap to the more robust GA16DE 1.6L unit instead of repairing.
keyPoints: GA14DE overheating issues are well-documented in owner forums
Head gasket failure can cause coolant ingress and hydro-lock risk
GA16DE swap is popular due to compatible mounting and wiring
Rebuilt GA14DE units include upgraded gaskets if you prefer to retain 1.4L
items.cta: Compare GA14DE repair vs GA16DE swap costs for your Sunny

items.question: How much does a Nissan Sunny engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan Sunny engines typically range from £800-£2,400 supply-only depending on engine code, with GA16DE units at the higher end due to demand and GA13DE at the lower end.
keyPoints: Used engines start around £450 but carry higher risk and shorter warranties
Rebuilt units with upgraded timing components offer best value long-term
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£650 depending on garage location
All EngineMarket suppliers include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare live Nissan Sunny engine prices from UK specialists today

items.question: Will a GA16DE engine fit my GA14DE Nissan Sunny?
items.answer: Yes, GA16DE engines are physically compatible with GA14DE Sunny models, but wiring harness and ECU differences require adaptation for EFI systems to function correctly.
keyPoints: Engine mounts and transmission bolt patterns are compatible across GA series
EFI wiring harness and ECU must be swapped or adapted for GA16DE
Many specialists offer GA14DE-to-GA16DE conversion packages
Carburettor GA15DS swaps are simpler due to minimal electronics
items.cta: Get quotes for GA16DE swap-ready engines for your GA14DE Sunny

items.question: What causes loss of power and hesitation in Nissan Sunny GA series engines?
items.answer: Loss of power in GA series engines is commonly caused by crank or knock sensor failure, which forces the ECU to retard ignition timing as a safety measure.
keyPoints: Crank/knock sensors degrade with age and heat exposure
Retarded timing reduces power and fuel economy significantly
Sensor replacement is relatively inexpensive if caught early
High-mileage engines may benefit from rebuilt unit with new sensors
items.cta: Compare sensor-ready rebuilt GA series engines for your Sunny

items.question: Are carburettor Nissan Sunny engines better for swaps than EFI versions?
items.answer: Carburettor GA15DS engines are often preferred for swaps due to simpler wiring and no ECU dependency, making installation easier in older or modified Sunny models.
keyPoints: GA15DS carburettor avoids EFI wiring complexity and ECU compatibility issues
Carburettors require periodic adjustment but are easier to diagnose
EFI conversions offer better driveability but need specialist wiring work
Choose based on your technical skill and intended vehicle use
items.cta: View rebuilt GA15DS carburettor engine options for your Sunny

items.question: How long should a rebuilt Nissan Sunny engine last?
items.answer: A properly rebuilt Nissan Sunny engine with upgraded components addressing known failure points should reliably deliver 80k-120k miles with regular maintenance.
keyPoints: Rebuilt GA16DE engines include new timing chain kits addressing the weak point
Regular oil changes with correct specification extend engine life significantly
12-month unlimited mileage warranty covers early failures for peace of mind
Driving style and maintenance habits heavily influence actual longevity
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt Nissan Sunny engines with upgraded components

items.question: Can I put a turbo engine in my Nissan Sunny?
items.answer: While the factory E15ET turbo was offered in B12 models, aftermarket turbo conversions are possible but require significant fabrication, tuning and supporting modifications.
keyPoints: Factory E15ET turbo engines are rare and command premium prices
Aftermarket turbo kits require custom exhaust, fueling and ECU work
Reliability depends heavily on quality of installation and tuning
Consider a rebuilt naturally aspirated GA16DE for proven reliability first
items.cta: Enquire about specialist turbo conversion options for your Sunny
